sentence_reviews.text: Predicted value is matched to reward plus discounted future value; it operationally connects Reinforcement learning and Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, Bellman equations and temporal-difference learning chapters; AIMA Chapter 22 reinforcement learning and Chapter 26 planning/control.
sentence_reviews.notes: The Bellman/TD target is directly supported; the explicit bridge to dynamical systems/control is a synthesis across RL and control sources.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives is to connect Reinforcement learning, Dynamical systems & control theory to downstream capabilities including Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, value functions, TD learning, and actor-critic methods; AIMA RL and planning/control chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: RL/value/actor-critic links are supported; the graph-role language is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, actor-critic methods use learned value functions/critics alongside policy updates.
sentence_reviews.notes: Actor-critic methods directly use value/critic estimates, supporting the substrate relation.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, Bellman/TD learning as central RL material; AIMA Chapter 22 reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: Bellman and TD objectives are directly part of reinforcement learning.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, value-function/TD objective material; AIMA RL and planning/control chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependencies are supported, but the generic objective-mismatch limitation is not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives uses Reinforcement learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto RL book, Bellman equations, value functions, and TD learning; AIMA Chapter 22 reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources directly place Bellman/TD/value objectives inside RL.

sentence_reviews.text: Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives uses Dynamical systems & control theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA planning/control chapter and RL chapter; Sutton & Barto sequential decision/value-function material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Control/sequential-dynamics context supports the dependency, but the exact atlas rationale is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA decision-making and game-theory chapters plus reinforcement learning chapter; Sutton & Barto RL sequential decision material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Decision-theoretic support is plausible; game theory is not directly necessary for Bellman/TD objectives and is broader than the cited evidence.

edge_reviews.notes: Bellman/TD/value objectives are central RL topics; core strength is supported.
Sequential dynamics/control context supports the edge; core strength is defensible but not directly stated as a dependency.
Decision theory is relevant to value objectives, but game theory is only broadly related; common strength is plausible but not strongly sourced.
